title: acaulimalva richii
intro: Acaulimalva richii is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the hollyhock or mallow family. It is native to Hawaii, where it is also known as "ma'o hau hele", which translates to "yellow hau" in the native Hawaiian language. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The roots and leaves of Acaulimalva richii can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting skin health.
